dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7509 IH: Deterring Adversarial Access to Americans’ Data Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moran introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to deny certain tax credits and deductions to businesses that use foreign adversary-controlled technology, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Deterring Adversarial Access to Americans’ Data Act.

section.header: Tax treatment of entities using foreign adversary-controlled technology

subsection.header: Entities using foreign adversary-Controlled technology added to list of foreign-Influenced entities
subsection.text: Section 7701(a)(51) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (D)(i), by striking or at the end of subclause (I), by striking the period at the end of subclause (II) and inserting , or and by adding at the end the following new subclause:

subclause.text: which uses foreign adversary-controlled technology during the taxable year.

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (I), by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.text: The term foreign adversary-controlled technology means any information and communications technology or service that is—

subclause.text: designed, developed, manufactured, or provided by a specified foreign entity or foreign-influenced entity described in subclause (I) or (II) of subparagraph (D), or

dependent on, or interoperable with, any other hardware, firmware, operating system, middleware, driver, embedded software, cloud service, or network component described in subclause (I) for core functionality.

clause.header: Information and communications technology or service
clause.text: The term information and communications technology or service means any hardware, software, or other product or service primarily intended to fulfill or enable the function of information or data processing, storage, retrieval, or communication by electronic means, including transmission, storage, and display.

subsection.header: Bonus depreciation denied for foreign adversary-Controlled technology and property owned by prohibited foreign entities
subsection.text: Section 168(k)(2) of such Code is amended—

clause.text: by striking or at the end of subclause (III),
by striking and or in subclause (V) and inserting or, and
by striking and at the end of subclause (VI),

subparagraph.text: in clause (ii), by striking the period at the end and inserting a comma, and
by adding at the end the following new clauses:

clause.text: which is not foreign adversary-controlled technology, and
which is not property owned by a prohibited foreign entity.

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of clauses (iii) and (iv) of subparagraph (A).

subsection.header: Denial of full expensing of domestic research and experimental expenditures for foreign adversary-Controlled technology and prohibited foreign entities
subsection.text: Section 174A(d) of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.header: Foreign adversary-controlled technology; prohibited foreign entities
paragraph.text: This section shall not apply to any amount paid or incurred—

subparagraph.text: for the acquisition or development of foreign adversary-controlled technology, or

subsection.header: Modified calculation of adjusted taxable income for purposes of business interest deduction

paragraph.text: Section 163(j)(8)(A) of such Code is amended by striking and at the end of clause (v) and by inserting after clause (vi) the following new clause:

clause.text: any item of income, gain, deduction, or loss which is attributable to—

subclause.text: a trade or business owned by a prohibited foreign entity, and

paragraph.text: Section 163(j)(11) of such Code is amended by inserting and which items of income, gain, deduction, or loss are attributable to foreign adversary-controlled technology or a trade or business owned by a prohibited foreign entity under paragraph (8)(A)(vii) before the period at the end.

subsection.header: Denial of credit for increasing research activities in the case of prohibited foreign entities
subsection.text: Section 41(d)(4) of such Code is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.header: Restrictions relating to prohibited foreign entities
subparagraph.text: No credit shall be allowed to a taxpayer under subsection (a) for any taxable year if the taxpayer is a specified foreign entity or a foreign-influenced entity.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after the date which is one year after the date of the enactment of this Act.
